Review: I can only assume that most of the positive reviews are referring to a different product than the Uncle Milton 3D Star Theater that I got. Very poor product. Definitely returning this one for a refund.

Review: After using several tubes (to be sure I didn't just have a weak tube) I can report that these have nearly NO aroma at all while using step in tub OR when you open them to put them in! Next to ZERO aroma. Period.  So, I guess I shouldn't be surprised it is NOT dissipating thru the tub air jet system.  I purchased both scents - the lavender and the eucalyptus. "Aromatherapy" and you would THINK there would be an aroma.  It is so faint that I feel like just throwing them away.  What a waste of time and money.  Just FYI - you must use the "air" on your tub to "get the benefit of the aromatherapy" - so YES I did properly use the correct setting on my tub.  Eh.  My advice - get a good quality aroma therapy candle and light it.  Don't waste your money on these in my personal opinion.  Grrrrr.

I take a size 9 to 9-1/2 in women's and this large is for a 9-10. However, it feels like a wide width and are swimming on my feet. They fit better if I wear thick socks with these.

Because my feet move around in these slippers, I often find my heel hitting the top of the low back and that is uncomfortable. In addition, if I do not wear socks, the tag in each slipper irritates my arch because it sits directly underneath it. Of course, I could try cutting that out, but I do not know if the "nub" left behind will be even more irritating. Had I tried these in a store, I would not have purchased them. I would have liked them more had they had a full back and were easier to keep them on my feet (meaning were more narrow in width).

Review: Title: Shave bar has nice fragrance but lathers less than a bar of soap
Review: I never tried a shave bar before so I was excited to try this. Unfortunately, this reminds me of a low suds bar of soap. I could barely work up a lather with or without the badger brush. The skin on my upper lip and my chin did feel moisturized and super soft after I shaved with this but I still do not like this bar. First of all, the bar is square and does not fit as well in the bowl of a shaving brush set. Secondly, the "lather" is almost non-existent as shown in my photo. It is for these two reasons I'll pass on this no matter how soft it leaves my skin and no matter how wonderful the fragrance.

Review: Title: Very soft and stylish stretch jeans but about 2 sizes too small Review: These jeans would have gotten five stars from me had they fit but they are definitely about two sizes too small. This size 36W x 33L fits like a 32W x 32L.

I ordered these for my boyfriend with the understanding that if they did not fit him that they would at least fit me. They do not fit either one of us. He takes a size 36W x 30L. He does sometimes take a 38W. I take a 34W-36W depending on the cut. However, these did not fit him around the waist even though they stretch. I then tried them on and got them up fine but could not close the waist no matter how much I stretched them. Other than this, these are a beautiful pair of jeans. I love the color, the feel, the softness, and the stretch. However, these are definitely not true to size. At least the pair I got is not true to size. Even the length is wrong. A 33L on me usually hits the floor, this fell just a little bit below my ankle which is what a 32L fits like on me.

Review: Title: Not pink, doesn't plump.
Review: Based on other reviews, I thought this would be a great product. I was surprised and disappointed to discover that this shade is truly colorless on my lips, and it doesn't possess any noticeable plumping factor. Also, other reviewers mentioned that this burns quite a bit and I totally disagree. There is some level of slight burning but it's very subtle. I've tried other lip plumpers that work much better, such as the Physician's Formula Plump Potion. While very sticky, that particular gloss does do what it's advertised to do. The NYX Pump it Up gloss really isn't worth the money. Anyway, people looking for long-lasting plumping won't really get results from a lip gloss - that usually requires some sort of collagen injection, and I'm not about to go there. I'll just have to be satisfied with my less-than-Angelina pout and chalk it up to genetics. :-)

I just forcefully pried open the handle and then slid the roller onto the track on top. I just use the release button to release the roller when I want.

I utterly dislike this and feel it is a waste of money after having tried this.

LIKES
- None

DISLIKES
- It does not work. All it did was soak up lotion and did not move it around on my body.

- At $20.39 ($23.99 minus 15%) this is outrageously priced.

- The foam rollers will disintegrate over time just like rubber bands.

- The handle has to be pried open with force and it feels like it is going to break. When collapsing it feels the same way.

I cannot recommend this. Applicators like [[ASIN:B093BF3FB3 this]] are far superior to this one, last for years, and cost much less.

Review: Title: Not my kind of facial therapy but I really did like the refreshing results Review: There is one mask in this pouch sandwiched between two protective sheets that have to be removed. Once removed, the mask is a gel-like sloppily-wet substance. I wish I had another person to put this on me. Nevertheless, I was able to flatten it out and set it on my face. I waited the requisite 20 minutes before removing it and then let the remaining solution soak in as per the directions.

The results were wonderful in the sense that my face felt refreshed and soft. However, I absolutely hate having to go through this. First, there is an ick factor to me with regard to the mask due to its slimy feel. Second, I am not a person who relaxes well; having to lay down and do nothing for 20 minutes other than relax is almost torture to me. Obviously, this is not my kind of therapy. I am glad I tried it but it is not something I want to repeat even though I am rewarded with a rejuvenated face.

<|channel>thought

Review: Title: Face tools are too tiny and hard to be effective; brush cleaners are okay but unnecessary Review: I have really been getting into silicone spatulas/brush sets for applying face creams, bacitracin on a scar, etc. However, this set comes with two silicone face cleaners on a "stick" and two brush cleaners. The face cleaners are way too tiny, hard, and inflexible to be useful. Perhaps they can be used to apply a pimple cream in spots, but other than this, I find them useless.

The two brush cleaners, which look like covers for the face cleaner, are okay. The purpose is to soap up a make-up brush to be cleaned and then rub it on the ribbed side of the silicone cleaner. It provides a good surface against which to agitate brushes to be cleaned. However, I find this unnecessary as using this requires I now clean the silicone cap and I am able to get brushes clean just by rubbing them with soap and water between my hands.

In summary, the brush cleaners are useful but I do not care for them and the face cleaners are useless. I personally hate this set.

Review: Title: Definitely worked to smooth my skin but left me looking windburned Review: The directions say to use this morning and night. Because I know I have sensitive skin, I only used this in the morning for almost 9 days. After the 9 days my face was smooth and it definitely worked as designed. However, almost my entire face looked windburned or as if I was out in the cold for a few hours. It is mostly for this reason I do not like this and will not be using it. The other reason is that $115 is a bit steep. However, for someone who does not have a reaction to this, this product might be worth a try over more radical beauty treatments. Unfortunately, this is not for me.

On another note, this is appears to be fragrance free to my nose. I prefer a highly perfumed product but for those who do not like fragrance, the aroma is barely detectable.

Review: Title: Stay away from these refurbed cartridges!!
Review: I opened one black cartridge, and it didn't print at all, even after numerous attempts to print and align the cartridges.  I then opened the second black cartridge and found that black ink was leaking out around the print head.  I am certainly not placing this in my printer to leak all over the inside of it.  I don't know about the color cartridge because I'm not even going to open it.  I tried to return these cartridges but found the time limit for returning is passed.  I order my cartridges whenever I put in the last one I have on hand -- how do I know how long until I'll need another one?!?  I didn't realize there was a time limit for returns but here I am one day past the time limit so I'm out the $.  Guess I've learned my lesson.  Here I sit with no cartridge for my printer, in the middle of projects.  Don't depend on these refurbed cartridges!!!

Review: Title: Low-budget, cheap brushes – one brush fell apart during first use Review: The picture on Amazon looks better than in person. The green is not as vibrant. I also feel the current price of $9.99 is outrageous for this set. 

There are 11 brushes in this set. The brush heads are very soft against my skin and they work like ordinary make-up brushes. I ordered this set because I wanted some very thin bristle brushes and this set has seven of those which is perfect for eyeshadow and the lips. The bristles also stay in the brush, at least so far, which is good.

However, the brushes feel very cheap and the handles are in two pieces. This means they will have a tendency to come unglued and fall apart. The first brush I was going to use fell apart in two pieces (see photo); I had to glue it back together.

In summary, I am not impressed with this set. I do not like how the color is not as vibrant on the Amazon site. The bristles are okay but the handles are not great and can fall apart.

Put this mascara on before puting on liner. I got a bit of mascara on my eye lid, wiped it off, but and when I did put
Liner on, it would not go on where I wiped the mascara off!!
Never thought my eyes were sensitive to eye make up, but my eyes are a Little itchy the next day.

This is not my favorite mascara, but better then most.
3 1/2 stars..

Review: Title: Basic foundation set; definitely NOT giftable - 2.5 stars Review: These arrived all squished in a plastic bag. In fact, I could not get the creases out of the brush or sponge. This set includes three synthetic blush brushes, two larger hand held kabuki brushes for foundation that each come with their own case, four blender spongers each with a flat angle, and one oval powder brush which is my favorite.

The blenders are good with liquids and the rest of the brushes are good with powder. I like the larger brushes for dusting on translucent powder or bronzer. I prefer using everything but the blenders with powders only.

This is a basic set but the presentation is awful and definitely not giftable.

For someone who likes to change their brushes and blenders frequently, this is an okay set. However, the current price of $21.98 is too pricey for this as it comes to $2.20 per item for this 10-piece set. I expect better for that price.

Review: Title: Functional, but poorly designed Review: This Belkin 8-outlet surge protector for the most part serves us well for plugging in our TV & entertainment center. I would have liked to use it for my desktop but the design has some flaws. Namely, anything with a DC converter ends up taking extra outlet space so I cannot use it & have every thing for my desk properly plugged in.

Like other reviewers have mentioned, there seems to be issues with the USB charging ports. I don't have much experience charging via USB port other trying with my computer. Attempting to charge my tablet via the USB port on my computer and the Belkin surge protector were equally disappointing. Slow as molasses in the dead of Winter. Is this a common issue or am I just unlucky? I'm going to go back to just using my regular charging cord & the outlet and not bother to try again.

In any case, I'm rather disappointed in this surge protector. Slow charging, poorly designed outlet spacing, & a high price makes it one that I would not recommend.

Review: Title: Awesome Features-Battery Life SUCKS! Review: I love (almost) everything about this tablet. Its the perfect size, comfortable to hold, has an amazingly clear/bright screen, Alexa, cameras, contains great apps like Kindle, Zoom, Netflix, Prime Video, etc. But, for the life of me, I dont' understand how it can possibly provide 'up to 7 hours of watching video, reading, browsing the web'. The battery in this tablet drains like wildfire. We lost electric the other night. I figured it was the perfect time to read a book I'd been wanting to start. When I read the first page, the battery level was at 94%. 2 hours later, 23%. Not long after, the battery was gone and i had to stop reading. I was not using any other app.

This tablet is such an improvement over the one I'd been using. Except for the darn battery life. Its awful. Its also the reason this isn't a 5 star review.

Review: Title: Fragrant steamers when dry but not so much when wet; lasted entire shower & left no stains Review: There are 15 shower steamers in a lovely lavender scent in this package. All are 30g (i.e. 1.058 ounces) tablets. They smell great and I love that they are all white because they do not leave stains.

I put this in an almost direct stream of water when it would not work in an indirect stream or on the side wall. Normally steamers are supposed to be out of the direct stream of water; however, I smelled nothing then. Although there was some scent once it started dissolving, it was barely detectable once I began my shower with my scented soap and shampoo. I took a shower for about 7 minutes and some tiny steamer pieces were left behind for the next shower.

The current price of $29.98 comes to an expensive $2 per steamer.

UPDATE: I found a way to get these to work better. I just put 3 to 5 steamers at the end of the shower out of the direct stream and the fragrance is more noticeable. I thought one would do it, but it doesn't.

<|channel>thought
1. Clues: 'lovely lavender scent' (positive), 'smell great' (positive), 'love' (positive), 'do not leave stains' (positive), 'smelled nothing' (negative), 'barely detectable' (negative), 'supposed to be out of direct stream' (negative, requires misuse to function). 2. Reasoning: The core function of a shower steamer—fragrance during showering—fails under normal use conditions. The positive attributes (dry scent, no stains) are irrelevant to actual performance. The reviewer had to misuse the product (direct water stream) to get minimal effect, and even then fragrance was overwhelmed by other products. Review: Title: Didn't last long
Review: Although I love the performance and features of this mouse, it doesn't last very long.  The small plastic feet on the bottom gradually slip loose which causes problems with the tracking - makes the device very irritating to use.  Logitech should sell replacements, but as far as I could find, they don't.  Also, the side button (the one below the forward/back buttons) seems to wear our faster than the other buttons.  I've owned at least 5 of these over the last 10 years and on 3 of them, the side button failed.  On one of them, I actually wore a hole in the plastic.  Not sure if this says more about the quality of the device materials or the force with which I click it.

Review: Title: eh
Review: As reported by many others, this robe runs very small.  I normally order a 14/16, 1x.  I ordered the 3x which fits like a 1x... sort of. The under arms are really huge.  Also, it's like it was a "second".... there was a "tie" on one side of the robe (meant to secure to another tie on the inside, keeping the robe closed) BUT the inner tie was not there.  Disappointing.  The outer tie is sewn on as reported by others and is not too high for me... I am 5'8".  The length hits me right at the knees.  I am going to keep it as I plan to use as a swim suit cover up but it is not a really awesome robe.

Review: Title: This is a bare bones basic curling iron; good for tight curls on very short hair; kickstand lousy Review: I have short to medium fine curly hair and this iron allows me to get a slightly tighter curl. However, it is a bare bones model. There is a light telling the user when it has reached a certain temperature (meaning it stopped blinking). The temperature control goes from 225 to 450 degrees Fahrenheit. The kickstand is awful as it keeps collapsing. Therefore, it is important to wear the supplied glove when using this. I do love the 360 degree swivel cord ability as it keeps it from tangling.

In summary, this is perfect for short hair and makes my curls tighter because of the small barrel. If one is looking for a tight curl and wants a super basic curling iron, then this is it. The current price of $24.99 is competitively priced.

Title: Brushes & combs are excellent; plastic rake is useless but everything stinks of burnt rubber Review: At the current price of $18.04 ($19.99 minus 5%), this is competitively priced for a total of five items. Included in this set are one large boar / nylon bristle brush with wooden handle, one small boar /nylon one small wooden comb, one plastic brush cleaner rake, long wooden rat tail comb, and one wooden wide-tooth comb.

Both brushes glide through my fine curly short to medium-length hair with ease. They are easy to hold; even the small one. The wooden combs work well too but I am not a user of combs.

The only item that is absolutely useless is the plastic rake. I was unable to get any hair out of the brush using this. Nevertheless, the other items are great, particularly the brushes.

The biggest problem I have with this set is that it stinks of burnt rubber. It is for that reason only I rated this 3 stars.

Review: Title: Thick Cream Doesn’t Moisturize, Absorb Quickly Review: This O’KEEFE’S HEALTHY FEET NIGHT TREATMENT is a thick cream that squeezes out in a thin (about 1/8 inch wide) strand--probably so that you won’t be tempted to use too much. The directions say to start with a small amount because a little is all you need.

My experience is that I need quite a bit of cream for each foot, because the product isn’t absorbed quickly. Also, the thick cream doesn’t seem to be as moisturizing as other overnight foot creams that I’ve tried. I don’t feel like the cream softens the hard areas of skin on my feet as well as other products. The cream has little noticeable fragrance, which is good.

I think that I’ll be using up a tube of this product very fast, which will make it kind of expensive for regular use.

Review: Title: Definitely not for me, but maybe for my sons
Review: First for pros: This is tiny, maybe about 3/4 inch thick at it's widest. Sleek, attractive and would probably delight any techie who is looking for some kind of new hip gadget. For the cons: It's too small. There are no indentations to grip the mouse and it is difficult to click on any area that you are trying to. Navigation/movement of the mouse is ok, it's the clicking that is difficult to do (and it becomes quite annoying to say the least). Within using this for a full day I, unfortunately, reverted back to my old wireless HP mouse. Maybe I am just old school and set in my ways (I am in my 50's), so I will be 'loaning' this to my techie son to give it a go. Maybe he will appreciate this better and will be able to navigate easier than I could. If that is the case, I will revisit my review and update accordingly.

Review: Title: Pretty “Golden” Watch, Sized for Woman’s Wrist Review: This TIMEX WOMEN’S CLASSIC DIGITAL MINI is a very pretty “golden” watch that is sized for a woman’s wrist. I love the way it looks.

It comes with the watch already running, so you’ll need to replace the battery sooner rather than later. You’ll also have to grapple with the instructions for setting the time, which are only available online. The instructions for the “Digital Mini” are the correct ones.

The instructions could be clearer, although they do work. They don’t tell you how to switch from 12-hour to 24-hour mode, if your watch gets switched to that by mistake (as mine did). I fumbled with the Mode button until I finally got the mode switched back, though I’m not sure exactly how I did it.

The buttons are hard to press--a real negative if you want to use the chronograph or calendar function a lot. The hard-to-press buttons also make it hard to set the time. You will probably find setting the time and date to be easier if you use something hard to press the buttons for you.

<|channel>thought
1. Clues: Positive: 'very pretty', 'I love the way it looks'. Negative: 'need to replace the battery sooner', 'grapple with the instructions', 'instructions could be clearer', 'fumbled with the Mode button'. 2. Reasoning: Mixed but balanced review. The aesthetic appreciation is genuine, but significant usability frustrations with battery and confusing instructions create substantial dissatisfaction. Neither positive nor negative dominates. 3. Verdict: Neutral sentiment.<channel|>

Review: Title: Use other body lotion over this to absorb. Seems to work pretty good. Review: The Nivea oil to me is the same consistency as baby oil, and has a very soft lite scent that does not last long at all.
I have to be carful pouring a little bit into my hand, so the oil does not spill on the dressing room carpet.

When I came out of the shower, and dried off, I put it on my legs and body. It did not absorb as fast an other review has mentioned.
My legs looked like they had baby oily on them after 10 mins.
Then I used my regular body lotion all over this, it all absorb into my skin then.
I have noticed my skin did not seem as dry as it has been and felt smoother.

I can only figure out what 3 of the ingredients are. Which is Macadamia oil and Avocado oil.
Although they look like a chemical to me!
Also the cotton seed oil. If that is real cotton seed oil, it is full of sprayed chemicals!!

Because what this is made of, I can only give it a 3 star, plus I have to use other body lotion for this to absorb.

Review: Title: Spacious bi-fold wallet but this anthracite black shows all scratches Review: This is a bi-fold wallet with a removable ID window. On the left fold there is room for three credit cards and behind that bank of slots is a removable leather ID pocket that can hold one ID and two credit cards on the back side. On the right side is room for four more credit cards and one more behind that. This means 11 cards can be stored inside. There is also a double billfold.

The front of the wallet has a nice lion's head embossing on it.

The wallet also comes with RFID protection to protect the credit cards from scanners. However, I have no way of verifying if this works or not.

It also comes in an attractive giftable box.

In summary, the slots in this wallet allow for the easy retrieval of all cards. I do not care for this anthracite black  leather because it shows every scratch and finger pass on leather. The wallet is also thinner than some other men's wallets. I have no idea how much this will cost but if it is priced in the $10.99 range, then that would be reasonable as long as one does not mind how this style shows every blemish.

Title: Spacious bi-fold wallet but this anthracite black shows all scratches Review: This is a bi-fold wallet with a removable ID window. On the left fold there is room for three credit cards and behind that bank of slots is a removable leather ID pocket that can hold one ID and two credit cards on the back side. On the right side is room for four more credit cards and one more behind that. This means 11 cards can be stored inside. There is also a double billfold.

The front of the wallet has a nice lion's head embossing on it.

The wallet also comes with RFID protection to protect the credit cards from scanners. However, I have no way of verifying if this works or not.

It also comes in an attractive giftable box.

In summary, the slots in this wallet allow for the easy retrieval of all cards. I do not care for this anthracite black  leather because it shows every scratch and finger pass on leather. The wallet is also thinner than some other men's wallets. I have no idea how much this will cost but if it is priced in the $10.99 range, then that would be reasonable as long as one does not mind how this style shows every blemish.

Review: Title: These are really cute... but run VERY ...
Review: These are really cute... but run VERY VERY small.  I wear an 8 and the 10's are very snug.  They are knit, with some stretch, however, they are lined with a faux fur of some kind... so there isn't a lot of give.  The top of the foot is a bit too snug... but hoping with a little wear they will stretch some.  The bottoms would be ok to wear outside.. but dry only as the rubber/plastic bottom covers only the bottom of the slipper... so you wouldn't want to wade through any moisture... They are cute... Just wish they were not so snug. Also the gray is a bit lighter and maybe a bit more taupey than the blue gray of the photo provided.  I will update if new info would be helpful later.

Review: Title: More like around the house. Its a hangy material, but soft ! Review: Nice as a tunic, and cropped pants to wear around the house ,I like this. Its very comfortable, and soft.
But it is not light , airy, and soft for pajamas I think.
The material is heavy and very stretchy, kind of hangy, and is good for traveling, as this can roll up in a flat ball.

To be fair this is big on me. I wear a size 10, and this is a extra large patite. I’m 5’2..36C, If your busty there is plenty
Of room in the top, & arms ,and tummy .etc.
This top hangs down long on me.
The top has a nice slit on the top middle, but is not low cut

The pants have a wide elastic waist band very comfortable,with a string tie, I didn’t need the tie,
The legs are wide, and big, the length is shorter then the model.more med calf.

This is sewed pretty good .
This is cute, too bad its not a nicer material.

Review: Title: Runs Small
Review: I've always wanted a red wool coat. I think they look smart! I wear a size 18 women's or XXL. The XXL coat was too snug on me. I could get it on, but the arms were too tight and I couldn't get the coat closed. It wasn't as long as I expected, which was actually good. I thought it might be below the knee, but really it was about mid thigh. For reference, I'm 5'6". The material felt pretty thin and cheap to me. I've had several wool pea coats and this definitely felt sub-par compared to them. The red color is nice and looks like the pictures. I like that the hood is removable. There's a belt, too. I didn't see anything wrong with the construction. Overall, it was just okay.

Review: Title: Hand & foot moisturizing oil that works well; the warnings makes me pause Review: This looks like a face serum but is actually a hand and foot oil. I tried a tiny bit on my hands and a little goes a long way. It absorbs quickly and feels silky and non-greasy. It left my hands feeling smooth and hydrated. I like the results but the warnings make one question whether it should be used at all.

The ingredients are caprylic/capric, triglycerides, isopropyl myristate, Di caprylyl, carbonate, Argania spinosa kernel oil, Vitis vinifera seed oil, citrus limon peel oil, Lavandula angustifolia oil, Cymbopogon martini oil, Cananga odorata flower oil, Daucus carota sativa seed oil, limonene, linalool, and eugenol.

Precautions
- Not recommended during pregnancy.
- Avoid exposure to the sun after use.
- Keep out of reach of children.
- Avoid contact with the eyes. Rinse immediately in case of contact.
- Do not ingest.
- Store in a cool place & protect from light.

The current price of $17.99 for this 1.01 fluid ounce bottle with eyedropper dispenser is competitively priced since a little goes a long way.

The main reason I bought the thing was that I hope to add HBOMax to my lineup, since it went away on Roku. I still have no idea how to do that, since I have so far not found any options by which to search for HBOMax from within the Chromecast interface to download or select it as an option. I've tried to add my Pandora account, which login info works just fine on my laptop and smartphone, but the Chromecast interface rejects it as incorrect.

Still in the process of setup, but I will update later once I've figured this poorly designed mess out...

Review: Title: Very cute but fabric sheds onto other clothing
Review: I really wanted to give this sweater 5 stars because it fits perfectly, is the perfect length to wear with black leggings, is extremely soft and comfortable, and is a great twist on a basic grey sweater, but it sheds terribly. It leaves grey fuzz all over my black leggings and left fuzz on all my black clothing when I washed it. It says not to put in a dryer, but I tried drying it anyway in hopes that it would get rid of the excess fuzz, but it still shed onto other clothing. This is a moderately priced sweater so it shouldn’t have this problem. Pretty disappointed. (From my daughter)

My kids have different brand ear buds (one has Airpods and the other has Skull Candy wireless) and that is a feature that both of theirs have. Review: Title: Comfortable, Works Well (Update 12-19-2017) Review: Bought this for the rare phone call to thr county or government where I'd need to keep my elbow painfully bdnt while being on hold or transferred from person to person endlessly. I figured on these rare occasions a headset would be just the thing to ease my elbow pain (arthritis). A bit cheap but works okay. Sill have to see about longevity.

Update - 12-19-2017: The pair I bought this spring got stepped on and the plug was bent beyond repair. I ordered another set and they arrived promptly. In less than a year it feels like they changed the design for the worse and sadly raised the price. I wish I'd saved the old set to compare because these feel far more fragile and fit my head poorly. They still work for those rare times when I need to be on the phone for a long period of time and I want to spare my elbow the arthritis pain. I'm less impressed with this pair than the last but I guess they'll do for the little I need them I really didn't want to spend big bucks on a better set for the infrequent use they get. Hope these last.

Review: Title: Eight double-banded headbands; knot on top; not for me Review: The tie-dye colors and design look great in the package. However, all eight headbands in this package double banded which means they come to a knot on top. Those type headbands do not fit me well, nor do they look good on me.

I personally prefer single band headbands over double. The double bands create a thinner line across the forehead than the single banded ones. The colors are great and I would have rated these 5 stars if they were a single band. Unfortunately, I'm not crazy about a "knot" in the center of my forehead.

The current price of $13.59  comes to a reasonable $1.70 per band. These are definitely pretty and if a bump in the middle is what one is looking for, then these fit the bill.

Review: Title: Solid glides on and protects me for 12 hours until my next shower; wish fragrance was stronger Review: I love intense fragrance and specifically love men's deodorants WITH aluminum because they seem to be what usually works for me. I usually use nothing but Old Spice Swagger solid with the active ingredient of aluminum zirconium tetrachlorohydrex gly 17%. Rarely can I get by with a stick that does not have aluminum. However, this stick does not have that ingredient but still protects me for 12 hours until my next shower.

I take a shower every 12 hours because I sweat a lot, so I can never attest to protection beyond 12 hours. The sandalwood and black pepper fragrance is not as powerful as I would like, however. Nevertheless, this works.

The current price of $15 is really boutique pricing for this 3-ounce stick. Protection-wise this works even though it is aluminum free. This is also labeled vegan and cruelty free.If the fragrance were stronger, this would have received 4 stars from me.

<|channel>thought

Review: Title: Stings My Sensitive Skin
Review: I have sensitive skin and this caused some stinging on my face. I tried it a couple of times just to make sure that it wasn't a one time issue. It did not cause any redness or anything like that. The product has a unique scent to it. I can detect the pineapple smell, but there's something else, too. I am neutral about how it smells - it's neither good or bad in my opinion. Other than the stinging, I didn't have any other reactions. There's a little bit of grit to it, but it isn't harsh. It feels a bit sticky and I had to wash my hands after applying it. On the few times I used it, I didn't notice any "wow" results, but I am a regular user of all kinds of anti-aging products that my skin is in pretty good shape for my age. The bottle/dispenser is nice and it gives the feel of a high end product.

Review: Title: Pins have air space in-between; these are roller pins & not actual bobby pins Review: There are supposedly 100 pins in this package; I did not count them. I did not realize these had an airspace in-between the pins. These are the type of bobby pins used with rollers (a.k.a. curlers) and not the type for an updo. I tried this around a lot of hair and they would not hold because my hair is too fine. I cannot use these but because I did not pay attention to the photo, that is my fault. They hold the hair very loosely or cause the style to fall out when using loose pins like these. I think they are perfect for rollers, however, because they will go over the roller easily.

The current price of $3.83 comes to $.04 cents per pin.

Review: Title: Love the simplicity of this riser & that it unfolds; but it could be better Review: This laptop stand has a small footprint when it folds up and I love that. It unfolds easily and holds my LG 14" laptop fine. It will also hold a larger laptop. There is room underneath to store a keyboard or folders, etc. However, what I do not like is that the hooks that hold the computer on the stand are in the way of my wrists if I am typing using the laptop keyboard. I hold my hands/arms toward the outer edge of either side of my computer to type and these hooks land right in the middle of my wrist. Of course, this is not a problem if using a separate keyboard.

The other issue is that the rubberized runners are just stickers they put on the bottom to keep the stand from slipping. The stand I received had the runners partially off in spots (see photo). I had to reset the "tape."

The current price of $19.99 is reasonable for this. I love this stand's simplicity and small footprint when not in use. It comes with a one-year warranty.

Review: Title: Just Okay
Review: I am a fan of the Tommy Hilfiger brand. I have ordered a few different bags in the past and this one has to be my least favorite. It's just okay. The bag just doesn't have the look or feel of the Tommy brand in my opinion. The quality of the bag is fine. The nylon bag is easy to clean. I don't care for the camouflage print but that's a personal issue. The straps are sturdy and seem to be secured well. I would prefer a little longer straps, but these do fit over my shoulder satisfactorily. I do like the inside lining. There are some inner pockets with one being a zippered one. There's a key strap so you won't lose your keys. There's an outer pocket as well. It's a good size tote, but my 17 inch laptop does not fit in it, though it was close. You could easily fit a tablet, magazine, book, and more inside. The only exterior branding is a TH tag clipped onto one of the straps. As I said, it's okay, I'm just not crazy about it.

Had I known this was so thin and that it would shrink, I would not have ordered this.

Review: Title: Not a product I will continue to use.
Review: This product seems to suffer from an identity crisis. The name says "AM Replenish," so maybe it's a morning moisturizer? But then it also touts its "Skin Firm Technology," so maybe it's care for aging skin? But "Primer" is in the name, so maybe it's for use under make-up? The "Drug Facts" on the tube, though, say specifically - "Purpose: Sunscreen," and to re-apply it every 2 hours (which, if I'm using it as a moisturizer or primer, why would I do that?). I just found the product & packaging either confusing or misleading. The one positive is that the light tint does blend in well with my skin tone, but unlike other reviewers, it felt heavy on me and I didn't like wearing it. This is not a product I will continue to use.

<|channel>thought

However, the files look terrible now because the glue and flecks of gold are left behind. They should have put the label on the back of the file or on the back of the black sleeves.

The current price of $9.45 ($9.95 minus 5%) is competitively priced for these files but I do not recommend ordering these as a gift until they get rid of that label, which just ruins the beauty of the file. There is nothing more irritating to me than a decorative and gorgeous file with what looks like a price sticker on it even though these files are functional and gentle on my nails.

Review: Title: Slow as molasses, but works as emergency back up Review: We were having drama with the Wi-Fi connection the first couple of weeks after moving into the new house. We got a new modem, all new Ethernet cables, and finally picked up this router thinking the problem was our original route. Turns out, the issue was a splitter on the cable company's end - whew.

This router is a good size and easy to use in almost any location. It sort of looks like the squatty third cousin to our Alexa. When we did have this unit connected it worked, but really struggled. Our usual router is a 2 channel set up and is just blazing fast. I knew this one wasn't on the same level before I bought it, but it is still considerably slower than expected. We have a thermostat, Alexa, two or three tablets, two smart phones, two TiVo minis and the laptops drawing from the WiFi at various times - a lot of stuff for this router to cope with. I've since gone back to our original router fortunately, but I am keeping this one as an emergency back up.

<|channel>thought
1. Clues: 'good size', 'easy to use' vs 'slow as molasses', 'really struggled', 'considerably slower than expected'. 2. Reasoning: 3-star rating captures mixed experience. Verdict: Neutral sentiment with balanced pros and cons.<channel|>

Review: Title: Aesthetically Appealing Shape Is Impractical for Cable Management Review: This AMAZONBASICS HIGH-SPEED 4 PORT USB 3.0 HUB (with AC Adapter for use with Macbook, Mac Pro, iMac, Surface Pro and more) works fine, and the round shape is aesthetically appealing. My experience with AmazonBasics products is that they generally perform reliably.

However, once I set this disc-shaped hub up, I realized that the port placement (at intervals around a flat disc) causes the USB cables for connected devices to take off in all directions, so that cable management in my computer area becomes difficult. What I do NOT need is another cable tangle that will catch tons of dust.

I can’t recommend this hub. A USB 3.0 hub with ports placed in a straight row is a better choice.

Review: Title: dickies heavy duty socks
Review: I was really expecting these socks (purchased for my big guy husband) to be a bit better quality as they are described as heavy duty compression.  They are not.  The first time he put them on, they snagged.  Eh.  They are not "compression" in any way - but they appear to sort of stay up - but if they were compression - they would definitely stay up.  They are cotton socks - but far from heavy duty work socks.  They will last for a few washes as far as I can see.  Disappointed.  We are using them right now as holders for ice packs for knees and elbows.  Not exactly what we'd hoped for using them for (on work feet!) but we have repurposed them.  I just cannot recommend them as heavy duty work/compression socks for men who really work.

Review: Title: I'd give it 3.5 stars but no 1/2 star option. Review: Just got the keyboard
I like the individual letter buttons, but there are some drawbacks.
Chord is shorter by a foot than other keyboards.
If you like they keyboard raised, you need to add something underneath as the built in raise is barely noticeable.
The 4 media keys (back-stop-pause/play-forward) share keys with F9-12 & you need to hit Fn at same time (which is annoying)
The other volume media keys are fine & above number area.
Length & width are great & it works fine, but keys are a bit noisy (if that matters)
I'd give it 3.5 stars but no 1/2 star option.

I cannot comment on mouse since it was not in box when delivered.

Review: Title: Back brush is best of the set; puffs are tiny; blue scrubber not for me Review: This set comes with a wooden handled back brush that has a brush on one side and massaging nubs on the other. There are also two buff puffs included. The buff puffs are so small they remind me of the freebies one receives with a liquid soap gift set. Also, included is a silicone scrubber with handles that has different sized nibs on either side.

The silicone item is too rough for me and it is awkward to use on the back. This might work well for some but it is not for me. The back brush is nice but I only use the brush side and I wish the handle was a few inches longer. I do not care for the massaging end. The buffs puffs would be great if they were bigger. I love mesh shower puffs in general but not tiny ones like these.

I have no idea how much this set will cost but I personally recommend buying the items separately unless the entire set will be used. Also, except for the back brush, the items are not high quality.

Review: Title: AmazonBasics Wireless Keyboard and Mouse Combo - Full Size - US Layout (QWERTY)
Review: Keyboard measures 18.3 by 7.3 by 0.8 inches. First wireless keyboard for me and the setup instructions were basic. There is no power switch for the keyboard. You put the mouse dongle into your computer, make sure Bluetooth is set to 'on',  and it will 'pair'. Why I am giving the gorgeous keyboard 3 stars is that the keys are 'sticky.' I am not one who pounds their keyboard and that is what you have to do. For the oldsters out there it is 'stickier' than an electric typewriter. I find myself tapping the keys and missing letters. The stickiest is the space bar. Final analysis is that it is usable just not as responsive as I am used to with the wired keyboards I have used.

Review: Title: 20 tiny polyester feeling scrunchies; not fo rme – 2.5 stars Review: There are 20 variable design scrunchies in this package. The material feels like polyester, so it is a bit slick which increases the chance these will slip out of the hair. I have medium length hair in the back and these held for the most part. However, I do not like the feel of these at all and the designs / colors are unexciting.

These come to $.50 cents per scrunchie at the current price of $9.99 so that is competitively priced for the quality received.

Review: Title: I did not notice any difference to my face or neck after using this Review: There is a small tin of gel mask in this set along with the eyedropper serum. I do not like the gel mask at all but that is because I do not like gel masks in general. Therefore, my review rating is based on the serum.

The mask takes about 30+ minutes to dry and is not easy to peel off. So this is not for me.

The serum is not easy to get into the dropper. I had to try a couple of times. Nevertheless, I tried this for several days over my face and neck and under my eyes. It is safe for the eye area. My face felt smooth but did not look tighter or any different. I also did not like that I had to add moisturizer on top of this. I figured this would suffice but that is not recommended.

Overall, this is not for me. The current price of $46 is pricey for something that I do not feel delivers the results I expected. It is just okay.

Review: Title: Sadly, Not Nearly As Effective As Canned Air. This Has Weak Air Blowing Power In Comparison. Review: I assure you, if you do a side by side comparison between this O2 Hurricane Special Edition and a typical canned air duster you will see a huge difference, and you will be disappointed like I was.

Dusting off a TV or computer screen or eyeglasses is a good test with this and the canned air side by side, of course, but you could simply just shoot the air on your hand and feel the inferior force from the O2 Hurricane Special Edition.

I was so hoping the O2 would be better than it is because like everyone I dislike the possible frostbite situation when using the canned air. Plus, the ability to use the O2 from any angle is really nice.

One day I do expect they will be able to make a canless air system in this kind of compact cordless size that has the air blowing power we need. But until that day, unfortunately, this one just isn't it.

Review: Title: Beautiful brush with an ordinary brush head; way too $$$
Review: The brush handle on this brush is beautiful and feels like it is handmade. The wooden handle is easy to grasp and hold during styling. However, the brush head itself is ceramic with typical prickly nylon bristles. This does work well to reduce frizzing and static during blow drying. Although this brush works well, I strongly feel the price of $50 is way too expensive. I know ceramic brushes cost more but it should not be this much more as there are others on the market that I have tried which are excellent and are much cheaper. The price is the only reason I rated this 3 stars.

Review: Title: Subtle & nice fragrance; lasts entire shower but scented shower products overpower the scent - 2.5* Review: There are 12 square shower steamers in this package. Six are citrus and six are lemongrass aroma. The instructions say to put this on a ledge outside the direct shower stream. I did that, but the fragrance was subtle. I then put this in an indirect stream of water from the shower and it was not much better. However, once I used my scented shower products no fragrance was detected. I find that to be the case with 95% of the shower steamers I tried. In fact, I have yet to find one that I love.

The fragrance of both is nice but fleeting. I am glad the color is non-staining. At the current price of $28.47 ($29.97 minus 5%), this comes to an expensive $2.37 per "steamer tablet." At this price money quickly washes down the drain. The one thing I do love very much about this, however, is that each steamer is individually wrapped and also shrink-wrapped inside that. This means these steamers can be used as stocking stuffers or for an Easter basket.

Review: Title: Not a Creamy Mousse
Review: This is not a creamy mousse. It does foam a bit, but the foam dissipates almost immediately when you touch it. I've tried shaking it and shaking it and that doesn't change the consistency. I have found that it works best when I apply it directly to my roots, rather than to my hands first. Then I work it into my hair. It does seem to give my hair some lift to it. It does leave my hair a little crunchy where it has been applied. It doesn't irritate my scalp or burn my eyes. Overall, it's okay. However, at the price point, it's not something I would continue purchasing.

Review: Title: Large rustic-looking handbag of simple design Review: This review is for the burgundy-colored bag.

This bag does not WOW me. It is fully functional with two outer pockets, a central compartment with one tiny zippered compartment inside. The strap is large enough to be worn crossbody. It is larger than I expected and a bit on the rustic-looking (used) side and is simply designed. I think the current price of $44.99 is competitive for a leather bag.

The straps are stiff and sides are a bit rough which makes wearing this crossbody for a long time when full a bit uncomfortable if one is wearing a very thin garment. Also, the card that came with the bag (last photo), says that "contact should be avoided with light colored fabrics as color transfer might occur..." which is disappointing to me.

Overall, I do not feel this is a great bag; however, it is extremely functional. I would not have ordered this had I been able to touch, feel, and examine this purse prior to ordering as it really is not "stylish" enough to me and has too much of a used look for my taste.

Review: Title: Could’ve been perfect Review: This product is almost perfect and probably could be if they fixed two issues. The first, and main, issue I faced was the microphone quality. I was extremely disappointed in the quality. People have repeatedly commented that I am very scratchy on calls and I’ve recorded myself multiple times on Voice Memos using these and the quality was subpar. They actually had worse quality than my iPhone’s built-in microphone and even the Lightning Connector Earpods that came with my phone. I’ve tried troubleshooting this multiple ways including, forgetting the device completely and pairing it again (exactly according to instructions in box) and turning my phone completely off and on again to no avail.
I also found that the volume level was somewhat lower than I expected. It isn’t extremely quiet, but to me it just seems a bit low.
Now, I don’t know if I am just unlucky and I’ve received a dud or if my expectations for this product are unreasonable, but I’d just like to see these problems resolved if not for my sake, then for the sake of others.
Other than those issues, I love the noise cancellation and the battery life is phenomenal.

Review: Title: Not For Larger Wrists
Review: It's been a while since I had gotten a new band for my Fitbit Versa 3. I was excited to have 6 new looks! The bands are easy to swap out. They snap into place nicely. The quality is good. I like the variety of prints. I have a larger wrist. While these can be adjusted to fit me, I don't like that in order to do so, the buckle gets moved up right next to the face of the Fitbit. (see picture). Fitbit recommends that the watch band is loose enough that when you shake your wrist, the band moves a little bit. You don't want it to move too much, but it needs to be somewhat loose. I had to adjust it to the max in order to get it to fit loosely. And if you have a larger wrist then I do, it won't work.

Review: Title: Thin, Very Wet Body Cream With Neutral “Cold Cream” Fragrance Review: PRE DE PROVENCE ULTRA-HYDRATING EAUX FRAICHES BODY CREAM FOR MOISTURIZING DRY SKIN (Oh Fresh) is a thin, very wet body cream with a mild, neutral “cold cream” fragrance. Unfortunately, the fragrance hangs around, and on my skin it quickly begins to smell unpleasantly “soapy”.

It does NOT irritate my sensitive skin, and it does make my dry skin areas feel silky smooth. A little goes a long way in coverage, probably because it's designed primarily to hydrate skin. It seems to hydrate my skin really well.

However, my personal preference is for a thicker body cream (e.g., Lubriderm) that gives the impression of providing deeper moisturizing and leaving behind a layer of skin protection.

Review: Title: Too small
Review: This is a sturdy and well made little case. But that was my main problem.. it was too small. I purchased this case to use with my new [[ASIN:B005MTMFHU Canon SX40 HS 12.1MP Digital Camera with 35x Wide Angle Optical Image Stabilized Zoom and 2.7-Inch Vari-Angle Wide LCD]]. This case was absolutely too small to carry the camera, extra batteries and the battery charger. I returned the Ape Case to Amazon and purchased the [[ASIN:B0000CCVC9 Canon 100DG Bag for Canon SLR Cameras]] from Best Buy, mainly because my trip was closely approaching and I did not have time to have the new Canon case delivered prior to my departure. The Canan 100DG is much roomier and allows for a ton of space for everything I could possibly need to take along with my Canon SX40. If you have a smaller camera or are not going to need to haul all of your extra gear with you (say, going out for a day long hike, etc.), then this Ape Case may fit the bill for you. If not, then I highly recommend purchasing a larger case for your camera and all the related goodies.

Review: Title: Thick conditioner; smells like Twizzlers

These are well made and a light material, terrific for layering. I favor the black one which I wear with a grey sweater and it looks great. Does not cling to my little  occasional 'muffin top' and gives me plenty of room to breath. Great idea for layering in clothing.

Review: Title: I like it
Review: I have naturally med-dark blonde hair.  I first purchased this eyebrow marker in Arizona at an Ulta. Since I have all the stuff to dye my eyebrows, I was really happy to find something that was way easier. It will last for 18 hours or more, unless you are really sweaty (working out, etc) and then needs to reapplied.  However, it doesn't run, smudge or flake off during normal use. There are a lot of mixed reviews and I wonder it is a quality control thing... I still have the one I originally purchased at Ulta months ago... so when it quits, I will open this one purchased through Amazon.  I will update if there are any issues.  Honestly, I love the color... it's a bit dark, but I like that.  It's not too dark.  the other thing is application... you literally swipe it on... in hair motions... not a "drawing" motion or it will look like a painted on line.  The swiping action makes it look more like natural hair lines, even up close.  Like I said, if I have any issues with this one, I will update my review!

Title: Great sulfate free shampoo with a wonderful Herbal Essences fragrance!
Review: I get Keratin treatments a few times a year and I typically buy some pretty expensive shampoo to help retain and prolong the keratin. One thing I cannon use on my hair is sulfates (or it strips the keratin). When I saw that this Herbal Essences shampoo was sulfate free, I thought I would try it. I always loved Herbal Essences and used to have it exclusively in the shower back many years ago. This shampoo is wonderful, leaves my hair soft and shiny and smells wonderful (lightly coconut fresh fragrance) and I don't have to worry about stripping my hair. Highly recommend for anyone who wants the best for their hair!

Review: Title: 1" flat iron
Review: I bought this on a lightening deal during "prime" shopping day... It works well... the clips are handy if you have medium to thick hair (and I do) to help you get to the bottom layer first... the clips are plastic. It's a 1" wide flattening surface... which I find is easier to use than the wider ones - personal preference here. It heats up in a reasonable amount of time. The base of the cord is a swivel so that helps keep the cord from getting all twisted.  I use a reusable rubber twistie to tame the cord when it's in my drawer - this helps keep the cord healthy and not broken. Wish it had come with a travel bag!  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

Review: Title: Love this GPS!
Review: I hike at least once or twice a year, and usually into areas which are not clearly marked or travelled by many people. I am thrilled to have gotten this Magellan as a member of the Vine program. I used it this past weekend, just walking in my neighborhood park. Let me start by saying it is super easy to set up (batteries) and programming the destinations (although maybe it's simply because I have been using a variety of GPS systems over the past 5 years or so in cars and handheld). This item is perfect for carrying in a pocket, is very lightweight and is extremely user friendly. I used it locally here all last weekend (various places in NJ and Pennsylvania) and it functioned beautifully. Of course the true test will be when I travel to Montana in May and use it in the wilderness. But I am excited with its potential!!!

Review: Title: Great way to scrub and exfoliate!
Review: I have been using scrubs in the shower for the past few years. I have really dry, crepey skin and scrubs really help. I wasn't sure what to expect when I saw these cubes, couldn't figure out if I would be able to control it in the shower (trying to get it onto my legs and arms). I was pleasantly surprised, however, to see how easy it was.  As I begin to use it the cube stays in the palm of my hand and begins to 'melt' slowly as I moved it up and down my arms and legs. There was plenty to go around and it was super easy to use. My skin felt amazing after just one use. I really did like the fragrance, too. I will be buying a few more of these for myself and also as stocking stuffers for my two daughter-in-laws (a unique gift). Highly recommend!

Review: Title: Amazing sound from a small system!
Review: Powerful and attractive, the SC-AKX73 is an amazing table top stereo system. As other reviewers have stated, this is a 900 Watt system (according to the specs) and it is every bit as powerful as that. As an fyi, I have a living room in my home which actually has a two story ceiling in it, so lot's of open space. I only had this unit cranked about a third of the way and I can say the entire room was throbbing. Very, very nice and very impressed. Bass tones are amazing and highs can rival the more costly Bose systems. All in all, I highly recommend this system to anyone. For the price, you honestly cannot go wrong for the features and quality that the Panasonic brand brings.

Review: Title: JBL Balboa Sub10 is a worthy contender Review: Although the JBL Balboa Sub10 delivers very good performance for a relatively modest price, I would never refer to it as "the little subwoofer that could." That's because it's far from little; this thing is quite big by my standards. However, considering that the function of a subwoofer is to reproduce low-pitched audio frequencies, I'll accept that fact that it's the size it needs to be do its job well.

Because I live in a multi-family building and have neighbors with which I share walls, I have not been able to crank up the volume beyond medium. That being said, I have been quite pleased with the bass sound and have not experienced any distortion with this subwoofer at the volume range that I have used. Audiophiles who do not have cost as an object will no doubt want a more expensive subwoofer than the Sub10. However, for the rest of us who are just looking for a dependable, reasonably priced subwoofer for our sound systems, the JBL Balboa Sub10 is a worthy contender.

Review: Title: Great shaving kit but overpriced for what you receive
Review: This was a great old-school shaving kit that I got for my 35 year old son to try. He absolutely loved it but complained, as others did here, that the sizes of the product are super small and he didn't get more than 3-4 uses out of the products. Of course, the case and brush are still usable, but it is costly for what you receive. The products themselves are great, he loved the shaving cream and balm and I did go on Amazon and order him a full size version of the shaving cream as a gift for helping me review this. I would think that if the Art of Shaving was trying to entice more people into using their products that they would make this intro pack more affordable (less than $20 would be more like it). Deducting a star for the price, but the product and presentation itself were a big hit.

Review: Title: Great basic line razor!
Review: I had my husband use this for almost a month so this review is largely based on his feedback. First, it comes in an attractive box, perfect for gift giving. Second, it's reasonably priced as a baseline razor. There are far better models of electric razors on the market, but for more than twice the cost. This razor is lightweight and fits very well in his hand (ergonomically correct). He maneuvers it easily and the shave he receives is surprisingly as close as the more expensive Braun models. Although he said he will be primarily using his better models, he will keep this one around mostly for business travel (due to its compactness) and as a back up to his 7000 series. He highly recommends this model, especially for those men who have not used an electric razor in the past and want to try it.

Review: Title: julep oxygen nail therapy
Review: After nearly 20 years of wearing gel/acrylic nails, I need to stop.  I have tried a whole range of different products.  All claiming to help strengthen weak nails.  This one really seems to help.  I it doesn't "peel" up after only a day or two - which has been the issue for me with other products.  It seems to really adhere to the nail bed. I am older and as we age your nails change and are less strong.  So, I don't expect to have the nails I had in my 20's - 56 now.  I have been using this product for about 6 weeks and I continue to see improvement.  Now, honestly, part of this is due to the fact that I am out growing the torture of the gels, but I don't think I could have survived without this.  Also, my first purchase of this product was not from Amazon.  If you are looking for a way to help your nails be a little stronger, I would recommend this product. You will be able to see how it works after the first use.  Further, I had similar positive effects with other Julep products, like the smoothing base coat ( good for the age ridges I have - minimizes them nicely.)  I always update my reviews should new info seem useful.

<|channel>thought

However, it does not meet my expectations. First, the leather is very hard (not like a soft plyable upscale leather would be). Second, there is no leather aroma/fragrance to it. I have to assume it's leather (as the ad says it is) but I would never be purchasing this if I knew what it was like. The inside is constructed well and there are places to put credit cards along with your passport. As of this writing there is no price listed for this passport holder (says currently unavailable). If it's cheap enough, it might be worthwhile getting. Otherwise, shop around a bit.

Review: Title: Wonderful shampoo, helps prolong the time between expensive keratin treatments!
Review: I have naturally wavy, thicker hair. I always get keratin treatments several times a year to help remove the frizz and allow my hair to stay straighter longer. Since I used these keratin treatments I have to be sure to use sulfate free products to help the chemicals remain in my hair follicles as long as possible. However, sometimes the shampoos that the keratin manufacturers recommend can cost as much as the treatments themselves. I loved that when I found this Alterna shampoo it had everything I need to maintain my hair health. Vegan and sulfate/paraben free, this shampoo is safe to use on my colored and treated hair. My hair looks beautiful, clean, shiny and smooth. I highly recommend this shampoo for everyone!

Review: Title: Barielle ridge filler
Review: I purchased 4 different brands of ridge filler and so far this one is the one I like the most.  It says it has silk fibers - it looks a little off white in the bottle - goes on clear.  The fibers don't show.  With 2 coats is does about 3 out of 4 of ridge filling. I think it depends on how deep your ridges are.  It isn't matte at all, but not super shiny - more like a natural looking gloss you would get using a rub/polish stick on your natural nail.  It dries in a usual amount of time -Some of my nails have some pretty good ridges - and my nails are not super strong. I have had artificial nails for many years - first acrylic and most recently gel.  But with the virus I haven't had anything but regular polish for about a year.  Sometimes the ridges cause splits and and then snag clothes, tear and bleed. I miss having my nails covered and am hoping to find something that will allow me to survive until we are allowed mani/pedis again!  I just applied it tonight, so I will update my review if there is anything remarkable to report - either positive or negative.

Review: Title: Cruise Ready and Stylin'
Review: Bought this for my cruise.  I was looking for something that wasn't huge but could still hold a decent amount of stuff.  I was able to fit (without it even flinching) my SeaPass and Cash in the back pocket, separate from everything else so I wouldn't loose it. Then, I was able to fit a full normal size bottle of sunscreen, lip balm, a smaller sunscreen, sunglasses, and lotion in the large pocket.  It never looked bulky and it was perfect.  The strap is too small in my opinion to go around your body but it could be done, I just didn't like it since I wanted the freedom of it being at my hip and off my shoulders. It held up great for 7 days and I'm expecting to be able to use it on all my vacations now.

<|channel>thought
1. Clues: Title is enthusiastic, detailed positive functionality description, 'perfect', 'held up great', expectation of future use. Minor preference note about strap usage. 2. Reasoning: Comprehensive positive review with specific evidence of product meeting needs. The strap comment is a usage preference, not a product flaw. Strong endorsement with intent to reuse. 3. Verdict: Strongly positive sentiment with high satisfaction.<channel|>
{"sentiment": 2, "confidence": 0.86, "rationale": "1. Clues: Title is enthusiastic, detailed positive functionality description, 'perfect', 'held up great', expectation of future use. Minor preference note about strap usage. 2. Reasoning: Comprehensive positive review with specific evidence of product meeting needs. The strap comment is a usage preference, not a product flaw. Strong endorsement with intent to reuse. 3. Verdict: Strongly positive sentiment with high satisfaction."}

Review: Title: Nice product, but super expensive for the size
Review: Ok, so assuming money is no object, this is a decent product. Exfoliator works well (not really for the face, more for the neck, chest, arms and body). I do love the moisturizer (body repair) it's thick and creamy (almost reminds me of my mother's old Ponds face cream). I will say that my skin feels great after a couple of weeks of use. Didn't really have a crepe problem, but I do have dry skin and some fine wrinkles. This duo does the job. However, the 4 star rating is discounting for the cost. This is really a 'trial' size, yet it is over $60 in cost. You can do better on your own using other products (I love Ahava products as well as Olay). I say shop around a bit or wait for the price to come down a bit.

Review: Title: Expensive, but ok
Review: I was looking for a good night time retinol serum to use. I was hoping this would have been a better alternative. As a negative, the applicator pump is a negative. Several times it either didn't pump or, when it did, it sprayed sideways (landing either on my mirror or countertop). As a positive, the serum (which is an orangish color) does go on easy and is absorbed readily. My skin feels a little tacky after use, but I did see good results after using for a month now. I think for $70 you can do better.

Clues: Mixed review with significant negatives (faulty pump, expensive price, 'you can do better') balanced against clear positives (good results after one month, easy application, good absorption). Review: Title: Well made and multi-functional!
Review: This is an amazing two in one iron. I have naturally wavy hair and it never seems to do anything on it's own. I regularly use an iron to straighten it, but I had not tried curling it until I received this device. I absolutely was thrilled with the results (although I certainly need additional practice). I like that I can go from straight hair to curled hair with a simple device. This is well made, heats up quickly and fits well in my hand making styling a breeze. I highly recommend this to everyone looking for a diversified iron.
